misc.title: #ONEPIECE1188 Harald’s death made Imu scream in pain, confirming that the way to weaken Imu is to take down the Holy Knights. Sanji has to lock in and lock in FAST 🔥🧯 "LUCIFERU JAMBUUUUUU" 👑 WorstG

Sanji has to lock in and lock in FAST 🔥🧯 "LUCIFERU JAMBUUUUUU" 👑 WorstGenHQ (@WorstGenHQ) #ONEPIECE1188 Might be the only last hope for Elbaph, can't believe the memes might became true, also my theory about Zoro/Sanji could be legit right.. 😭 — https://nitter.net/WorstGenHQ/status/2074443931907977662#m

misc.title: #ONEPIECE1188 The theory does not claim that they have to fight Imu again; rather, killing Sommers and Kilingham would weaken Imu. We saw how deeply Harald's death affected Imu, he was gasping for bre
misc.text: #ONEPIECE1188 The theory does not claim that they have to fight Imu again; rather, killing Sommers and Kilingham would weaken Imu. We saw how deeply Harald's death affected Imu, he was gasping for breath and afraid. This could be the key to weakening Imu, as their deaths would have an impact on him. WorstGenHQ (@WorstGenHQ) #ONEPIECE1188 Might be the only last hope for Elbaph, can't believe the memes might became true, also my theory about Zoro/Sanji could be legit right.. 😭 — https://nitter.net/WorstGenHQ/status/2074443931907977662#m

misc.title: Trivia💡: In JP version, Oda wrote Zoro and Sommers’ talk as a carefully crafted battle of **sarcasm**.🔥 Zoro says that Holy Knights *interrupted* the feast, and Sommers sarcastically repeats the sam
misc.text: Trivia💡: In JP version, Oda wrote Zoro and Sommers’ talk as a carefully crafted battle of **sarcasm**.🔥 Zoro says that Holy Knights *interrupted* the feast, and Sommers sarcastically repeats the same word, using it like a rough version of “Sorry for *interrupting*.” Sommers then says that his *colleague* is taking the children, and Zoro answers by using the exact same word, *colleague*, against him. Another important detail is that Sommers uses some exclamation marks, while Zoro does not use even one. This difference makes Zoro seem much calmer and more controlled throughout the talk.😍💪

misc.title: 【入荷情報】 週刊少年ジャンプ 2010年 21・22号 入荷しました！ 巻頭カラーは『ONEPIECE』。Cカラーは『銀魂』『黒子のバスケ』。そして『HUNTE×RHUNTER』はあの伝説回が収録されています。「強制的に成長したんだ……ボクを倒せる年齢まで！！」 shop.kosho-mirokudo.com/item…

misc.title: According to author comment in chapter 818, Oda took up **calligraphy** back in 2016. The phrase “Monkey D. Luffy vs. Nerona Imu” is also written in an elaborate calligraphic style, with the lettering
misc.text: According to author comment in chapter 818, Oda took up **calligraphy** back in 2016. The phrase “Monkey D. Luffy vs. Nerona Imu” is also written in an elaborate calligraphic style, with the lettering itself treated as part of the design.😍

misc.title: Aight! Lets talk about it The premiere genuinely felt like watching an episode that came out just a week after #170. Recaps were tastefully placed in the middle of dialogues and not used for padding.

items.title: Trivia💡: In JP version, Oda wrote Zoro and Sommers’ talk as a carefully crafted battle of **sarcasm**.🔥 Zoro says that Holy Knights *interrupted* the feast, and Sommers sarcastically repeats the sam
items.text: Trivia💡: In JP version, Oda wrote Zoro and Sommers’ talk as a carefully crafted battle of **sarcasm**.🔥 Zoro says that Holy Knights *interrupted* the feast, and Sommers sarcastically repeats the same word, using it like a rough version of “Sorry for *interrupting*.” Sommers then says that his *colleague* is taking the children, and Zoro answers by using the exact same word, *colleague*, against him. Another important detail is that Sommers uses some exclamation marks, while Zoro does not use even one. This difference makes Zoro seem much calmer and more controlled throughout the talk.😍💪
